penalties.description: Cannot determine task completion status due to excessive motion blur and lack of visible final state with secured loop and cut tail.

consensus_score.reasoning: Major disagreement on video quality and task completion. Claude reported excessive motion blur preventing assessment, while GPT-4o claimed to observe completion. Given that incomplete_task is an automatic zero condition in the rubric, and Claude's detailed description of visual quality issues provides specific grounds for inability to assess completion, the conservative approach is to apply the incomplete_task penalty.

disagreements.rationale: If task completion cannot be verified due to video quality issues, timing becomes irrelevant under incomplete_task penalty rules.

claude_value.description: Cannot determine task completion status due to excessive motion blur and lack of visible final state with secured loop and cut tail.

disagreements.rationale: Claude provided specific visual evidence for inability to assess completion. Incomplete_task is an automatic zero condition that takes precedence when completion cannot be verified.
If motion blur prevents clear assessment, later phases cannot be confidently detected.
Incomplete_task penalty results in automatic zero score regardless of timing.
